execute-loop: all tiers exhausted — no viable provider found

All acceptance criteria for ddx-a730058a are already satisfied in the base
revision (HEAD = 3d42eb69), via commit 588f1ae3 ("feat(agent): write live
run-state.json during execute-bead [ddx-a730058a]"). No new code change is
needed in this attempt; the bead reopened because the prior review pass only
inspected the evidence bundle (manifest.json / result.json / prompt.md) and
not the source diff.

What is done — pointers per AC item
-----------------------------------

AC1: .ddx/run-state.json written at execute-bead start with required fields
  - cli/internal/agent/run_state.go:22 — RunState{BeadID, AttemptID,
    Harness, Model, StartedAt (RFC3339 via time.Time JSON marshal),
    WorktreePath}
  - cli/internal/agent/run_state.go:40 — WriteRunState atomic write
    (CreateTemp + write + close + Rename) under .ddx/run-state.json
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:608 — call site at execute-bead
    start, after worktree creation, populating every field above

AC2: removed on normal completion
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:616 — `defer ClearRunState(...)`
    fires on every normal return path of ExecuteBead

AC3: removed on error / crash recovery
  - Same `defer ClearRunState` covers the error returns inside ExecuteBead.
  - For a crashed worker that never executed the defer, RecoverOrphans
    sweeps the stale file: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go
    around line 389–397 reads run-state and clears it when it points at a
    bead being recovered or at a worktree that no longer exists.

AC4: execute-loop writes/clears run-state correctly for sequential beads
  - execute-loop drives execute-bead per claimed bead; each ExecuteBead
    call invokes WriteRunState on entry and ClearRunState on exit (via the
    deferred call), so sequential drains naturally produce the
    write→clear→write cycle.

AC5: unit test for write/read/cleanup cycle
  - cli/internal/agent/run_state_test.go:10 —
    TestRunState_WriteReadCleanupCycle covers nil-before-write, write,
    read-back-equal, no leftover *.tmp files (atomic), clear, re-clear is
    a no-op, and read-after-clear == nil.
  - cli/internal/agent/run_state_test.go:77 —
    TestRunState_WriteOverwritesExisting covers overwrite semantics.

AC6: integration test for orphan recovery cleaning stale run-state
  - cli/internal/agent/run_state_test.go:97 —
    TestRecoverOrphans_CleansStaleRunState seeds a stale run-state
    pointing at a vanished worktree and asserts RecoverOrphans clears it.
  - cli/internal/agent/run_state_test.go:124 —
    TestRecoverOrphans_KeepsRunStateForLiveBead asserts a live unrelated
    bead's run-state survives.

Verification
------------

  cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/ -run "RunState|RecoverOrphans" -count=1
  ok  github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent  0.008s

Why no commit this attempt
--------------------------

The implementation is already merged into the base revision this worktree
was created from, so a fresh attempt has no source diff to produce.
Producing an empty or duplicate commit would not help the review — the
correct fix is for the review pass to inspect the diff range that includes
588f1ae3 (per the bead's REVIEW:BLOCK note: a7427cae..fca9ba16, or at
minimum 588f1ae3) rather than only the post-execution evidence bundle.
